serving.natural_language_portion: One cup serving of approximately 120 g, comprising a layered vanilla sponge base, fresh strawberries, strawberry sauce, and brûlée custard cream.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake / cream cake composite — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Cake, shortcake, biscuit-type (NDB 18101) and Cream, whipped, cream topping, pressurized (NDB 01053), adapted for Hokkaido-style strawberry custard cup cake
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for a Hokkaido-style strawberry custard cup cake. Values are estimated by weighted composition of: (1) vanilla sponge cake (USDA SR Legacy NDB 18101, cake shortcake biscuit-type, ~50% by weight), (2) custard cream / pastry cream (USDA SR Legacy NDB 19170, pudding, vanilla, dry mix, ~25% by weight), and (3) fresh strawberries with strawberry sauce (USDA SR Legacy NDB 09316, strawberries raw, ~25% by weight). Weighted average per 100 g computed. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hokkaido strawberry custard cup cake. GI estimated at 62 based on sponge cake GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (sponge cake GI range 46–66, mean ~56–62). The custard cream and strawberry sauce components add moderate sugar load, supporting a GI estimate at the upper end of the sponge cake range (~62). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 41.4) / 100 ≈ 25.7, rounded to 25.6 for a 120 g serving (34.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.2 = 41.4 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~145 mg per 100 g) and added sugar contributing to caloric density
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small cup serving and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the cumulative sodium intake from a full dessert course may approach 200–250 mg per sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~20 g per 100 g) and medium glycemic index (~62) with a high glycemic load per serving (~25.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this dessert as an occasional indulgence only; the high glycemic load of approximately 25.6 per cup serving is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise, and portion control or substitution with a lower-GI dessert is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.8 g per 100 g) from custard cream and butter, combined with added sugars
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this cake due to its saturated fat and added sugar content, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles; a single cup serving contributes approximately 7 g of saturated fat, representing 35% of the recommended daily limit of 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Hokkaido Strawberry Cake (Cup) is a refined Japanese-inspired layered dessert sold in Singapore, most notably by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall. It consists of a light vanilla sponge cake base layered with fresh whole or sliced strawberries, a proprietary strawberry sauce, and a rich brûlée custard cream — a style of pastry cream associated with Hokkaido dairy traditions known for their high-fat, silky-smooth texture. The dessert is served in an individual cup format, making it a single-portion indulgence. The sponge is intentionally less sweet than conventional cream cakes, allowing the natural tartness of the strawberries and the richness of the custard to dominate the flavour profile. Texturally, the dessert combines the soft, airy crumb of the sponge with the smooth, dense custard and the fresh, juicy bite of strawberries. Nutritionally, each 120 g cup serving provides approximately 298 kcal, 41 g of carbohydrate, 12.6 g of fat (including 7 g saturated fat), and 24 g of sugar. The glycemic load per serving is estimated at approximately 25.6, placing it in the high glycemic load category. This dessert is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar and saturated fat content. It is vegetarian but not vegan, contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and is not certified halal. The dish reflects the broader Singapore trend of adopting Japanese patisserie techniques and premium dairy ingredients into the local dessert market.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Hokkaido Strawberry Cake (Cup) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for vanilla sponge cake, custard/pastry cream, and fresh strawberries. GI is estimated by analogy from sponge cake values in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composition of USDA SR Legacy components (sponge cake ~50%, custard cream ~25%, strawberries and sauce ~25%) as no direct database entry exists for this specific product. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 3. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 4. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on sponge cake GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this product. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the restaurant Kazo Premium - Funan is not listed as a halal-certified establishment on the MUIS Singapore halal food directory as of the processing date. 6. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for a standard single cup portion; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 7. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu title '2 x (Cup) Signature Hokkaido Strawberry Cake' to the canonical form 'Hokkaido Strawberry Cake (Cup)' representing a single cup serving.
